title: rubus caesius subsp. deltoideus
intro: Rubus caesius subsp. deltoideus, also known as the black-dragon or black-bryony, is a plant species within the Rubus genus. It is a type of blackberry that grows in the mountainous regions of Europe, characterized by its dark purple to black fruits and thorny stems. The medicinal properties of Rubus caesius subsp. deltoideus include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive health, and it can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use.
